journal_entry.description: Conversion of 50 preferred shares to 25 common shares
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, share conversion is an equity reclassification transaction. The carrying value of the preferred shares ($100 total, or $2 per preferred share) is removed from Preferred Shares (equity decrease, debit) and transferred to Common Shares (equity increase, credit) at the same carrying amount. This is a book transfer within equity with no gain, loss, or tax consequences. The tuple requires 1 debit line (equity decrease) and 1 credit line (equity increase), which matches this reclassification structure.

ambiguities.resolution: Convention states 'converted X to Y' means book value at stated amounts. The carrying value of $100 for the preferred shares becomes the basis for the common shares issued. This is a standard equity conversion treated at book value.

ambiguities.aspect: whether transaction involves a gain or loss
ambiguities.resolution: Equity conversions are recorded at book value with no gain or loss recognition. The $100 carrying value of preferred shares is simply reclassified to common shares equity accounts.

ambiguities.aspect: par value or stated value of the shares
ambiguities.resolution: Not material to the entry structure. The conversion is recorded at the $100 carrying value regardless of par values. Par value affects only the allocation between common shares and additional paid-in capital accounts, not which accounts are used or total amounts.

output.reason: Conversion of preferred shares to common shares: Debit decreases Preferred Share Capital (equity decrease, 1 line). The credit side will increase Common Share Capital. This is an equity reclassification transaction with no impact on total equity, assets, liabilities, or income.

debit_classifier.reason: Conversion of preferred shares to common shares: Debit decreases Preferred Share Capital (equity decrease, 1 line). The credit side will increase Common Share Capital. This is an equity reclassification transaction with no impact on total equity, assets, liabilities, or income.

output.reason: This is an equity conversion transaction. The preferred shares (carrying value $100) are being cancelled/reduced, which requires debiting Preferred Share Capital. To credit side: Common share capital increases by the same amount ($100). This is an equity-to-equity conversion with no impact on assets, liabilities, or income. Credit: Common Share Capital = equity increase (1 line).

credit_classifier.reason: This is an equity conversion transaction. The preferred shares (carrying value $100) are being cancelled/reduced, which requires debiting Preferred Share Capital. To credit side: Common share capital increases by the same amount ($100). This is an equity-to-equity conversion with no impact on assets, liabilities, or income. Credit: Common Share Capital = equity increase (1 line).
